thought: Blend the first-frame-locked mid-battle state with the scraped gameplay video's fixed side-view combat rhythm: start by resolving the already-visible allied hit on the right side, making the floating damage text fade while one enemy HP bar drops slightly. Keep the camera completely fixed and preserve all unit anchors from the first frame. Then borrow the reference video's short-prep, fast-trigger, hit-stop, shield/impact layering, and UI-led damage feedback for a right-to-left enemy counterattack. Motion should be small-displacement and trigger-based rather than cinematic long dashes: brief glow/charge on the purple bird or a small flying enemy, a compact projectile or lunge toward the center-left allies, concentrated hit flash, 0.05-0.12s impact pause, ally shake, damage number/status popup, then a quick return to idle round-4 combat stance. Use the provided assets for battlefield base, hero ultimate-style trigger accents, enemy hit reaction, ultimate/localized impact VFX, spirit beast assist traces if needed, and combat UI feedback, but do not turn this into a full-screen cut-in ultimate; it must remain readable as in-engine battle playback matching the reference video's timing and VFX logic.
prompt_en: First Frame: @0045.jpg. Reference Video: @trimmed_best_ref_1774779760.mp4. Motion Style Reference: fixed horizontal gameplay camera, short anticipation, fast trigger-based attack, concentrated impact flash, shield ripple, large damage text, brief hit-stop, quick return to idle combat flow. Preserve the exact first-frame composition: round 4 UI at top center, allied cluster on left-center and lower-left, enemy formation on right led by the large purple bird, misty mountain background visible, cracked ground platform unobstructed, no camera push or angle change, no full-screen cut-in. [00s-01s] Resolve the exact captured moment first: the existing floating damage text near middle-left begins to fade and drift upward, one targeted right-side enemy health bar decreases slightly, status icons remain locked above units, all characters keep their original positions with only subtle idle motion and minor recoil settling. [01s-02s] Following the reference video's 1-second establish-to-trigger rhythm, the right-side large purple bird or one smaller flying companion initiates a compact counterattack from right to left: a brief edge glow/charge appears around the attacker, wings or body make a small forward thrust without large displacement, and a tight projectile or slash-like energy streak forms along the middle lane toward the central-left allied cluster. [02s-03s] Impact lands on the targeted center-left ally with a localized bright flash from @ultimate_vfx_sheet_182541.png, layered with a compact spirit-beast-like accent from @spirit_beast_assist_fx_182626.png if helpful, then a visible 0.05-0.12 second hit-stop. During the hit-stop, the ally jerks backward slightly, the enemy side remains readable, and a shield ripple or spherical barrier reaction briefly appears around the hit area in the style of the reference gameplay. [03s-04s] Combat UI feedback peaks: burst out large damage numbers and possible crit/status text using @combat_ui_feedback_pack_182709.png above the struck ally, make the ally HP bar drop a bit, keep buff/debuff icons in place, add a short enemy-hit/ally-hit shake using frames from @enemy_hit_reaction_sheet_182456.png, and let the projectile flash dissipate quickly. [04s-05s] The battlefield rhythm tightens and settles just like the reference video after impact: all units snap back into their idle battle poses, the purple bird resumes hovering on the right, left-side allies recover from recoil, remaining small enemies continue subtle fluttering, and the UI stays fully readable with round 4 unchanged. [05s-06s] End on a brief active-combat hold ready for the next turn order advancement, with lingering glow fading, no victory/result screen, no scene transition, no dramatic camera movement, maintaining a clean in-engine battle replay look. Visual priorities: preserve first-frame layout exactly, keep motion compact and gameplay-authentic, emphasize UI-led hit confirmation over complex choreography, and make all effects readable within one locked side-view shot.
prompt_zh: > 首帧锁定: @0045.jpg。参考视频: @trimmed_best_ref_1774779760.mp4。动作风格参考: 固定横向战斗机位、短前摇、快速触发式出手、命中点集中闪光、护盾波纹、大号伤害字、短暂停顿、随后迅速回到待机战斗节奏。严格保持首帧构图与战场逻辑：顶部中央第4回合UI，左侧中部与左下为我方单位，右侧为敌方编队且由大型紫色鸟类敌人领衔，雾气山景背景与龟裂地面完整可见，不改机位、不推拉、不做全屏大招演出。 [00s-01s] 先结算首帧中已经发生的攻击结果：画面中间偏左已存在的飘字开始上浮并逐渐淡出，右侧一名已受击敌人的血条小幅下降，所有状态图标保持原位，角色整体仍停留在原始站位，仅有轻微待机与受击后摇收束。 [01s-02s] 按参考视频的“1秒建立后快速触发”节奏，右侧大型紫鸟或其下方一只小型飞行敌人发动反击，攻击方向必须从右到左：攻击者出现短促描边发光/蓄力，身体或翅膀做一个小幅前探动作，不进行长距离位移，同时沿中路打出一道紧凑的飞行弹道或斩击能量，直指左侧中间的我方单位群。 [02s-03s] 命中左侧目标时，在受击点叠加来自 @ultimate_vfx_sheet_182541.png 的局部爆闪特效，必要时辅以 @spirit_beast_assist_fx_182626.png 的短促灵兽能量尾迹，但规模必须控制在实机战斗可读范围内；加入0.05到0.12秒的命中停顿。停顿期间，我方目标轻微后仰抖动，受击区域短暂出现参考视频风格的球形护盾/屏障波纹反馈。 [03s-04s] UI打击反馈达到峰值：使用 @combat_ui_feedback_pack_182709.png 在被击中的我方单位上方弹出大号伤害数字，可附带“暴击”或异常状态文字；目标血条下降一截，增益减益图标保留；结合 @enemy_hit_reaction_sheet_182456.png 的抖动/受击帧表现，让命中确认清晰直接，随后投射物与闪光快速消散。 [04s-05s] 参考视频中“爆发后迅速收束”的节奏，战场回到稳定对峙：右侧紫色大鸟恢复悬浮待机，左侧被击中的我方单位从后仰中回正，其余友军与小型飞敌保持轻微待机动作，顶部第4回合UI持续可见，整体仍处于战斗进行中而非结算。 [05s-06s] 以一个短暂的实机战斗停顿收尾，准备进入下一次行动顺序推进；残留光效逐渐熄灭，不出现胜利字样、不切场、不改变镜头。重点要求：完全遵守首帧站位与横向战场轴线，动作以小位移触发式攻击为主，打击感主要依靠飘字、血条变化、命中闪光、护盾波纹和短停顿来建立，呈现接近手游战斗回放的清晰单镜头效果。
